sections.heading: Supported Stages
sections.body:
The MongoDB Search Playground supports a subset of aggregation pipeline stages, including:

- [$search](pipe.$search)

- [$searchMeta](pipe.$searchMeta)

- [$project](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/project)

- [$limit](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/limit)

- [$skip](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/skip)

- [$facet](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/facet)

sections.heading: Explain Search Queries
sections.body:
The MongoDB Search Playground includes an **Explain** feature to ease understanding of how search
queries execute. When running a query, switch to the **Explain** tab beside the
**Results** tab in the results pane to view an explanation of the query plan.

The **Explain** feature shows the query plan in two formats:

- **Textual Explanation**: A summary that describes the query plan in natural language,
  helping you identify performance or logic issues.

- **Explain Output**: The detailed JSON structure of the [explain helper](manual/reference/method/cursor.explain) output with the [queryPlanner verbosity](manual/reference/explain-results).

sections.heading: Considerations
sections.body:
MongoDB logs your workload data for monitoring the system health and to
help troubleshoot any issues in the MongoDB Search Playground.

sections.heading: Search Playground Limitations
sections.body:
- The MongoDB Search Playground only supports a single collection. You can't use [$lookup](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup) or
  [$unionWith](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/unionWith) to [search multiple collections](fts-cross-collection-tutorials).

- The MongoDB Search Playground environment does not persist. To save an
  environment, use the **Share** button to generate a snapshot
  URL (Uniform Resource Locator) that lasts 30 days. The snapshot functionality is only available
  for the **Code Sandbox** tool.

- The MongoDB Search Playground has the following data limitations:

  - You can't add more than 500 documents to the MongoDB Search Playground.

  - You can't import files larger than 100 KB.

  - Your total data in the MongoDB Search Playground, including the collection, search index, synonyms,
    and queries, can't exceed 300 KB.

- The MongoDB Search Playground doesn't support indexing fields as the [vector index type](bson-data-types-vector) type and running queries using the
  [vectorSearch](fts-vectorSearch-ref) operator.

- The MongoDB Search Playground is not connected to your clusters. However, you can
  recreate your Atlas environment in the MongoDB Search Playground by:

  - [Exporting documents](database-tools/mongoexport) from Atlas and importing
    into the MongoDB Search Playground.

  - Copying the MongoDB Search index from your Atlas **JSON Editor** and pasting
    into the **Index** panel in the MongoDB Search Playground.

sections.heading: Step 1: Access the MongoDB Search Playground.
Step 2: Choose your tool.
sections.body:
The MongoDB Search Playground provides a JSON (Javascript Object Notation)-only and visual way to
explore MongoDB Search features.

| Code Sandbox         | Provides a way to add your own data, define a MongoDB Search index in JSON (Javascript Object Notation) format, and construct and run $search and $searchMeta queries in JSON (Javascript Object Notation) format. This playground also includes several templates to get you started with MongoDB Search.You can also save and share your playground with others. This allows anyone to interact with your playground snapshot for up to 30 days. Any changes made after generating the playground URL won't affect the original snapshot. |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Search Demo Builder  | Provides a visual way to explore the following MongoDB Search features and query the data.Fields to indexSearch-as-you-type (autocomplete)Filters and facetsThe tool automatically generates your index definition and query syntax that you can copy and use in your Atlas cluster.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| Chatbot Demo Builder | Provides a way to try a RAG chatbot that answers your questions using MongoDB Vector Search. Try the chatbot with your own data or by using the sample data. To learn more, visit Chatbot Demo Builder.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |

Sample data is pre-loaded by default in the **Code Sandbox** and
**Search Demo Builder**. Alternatively, you can upload your own
data.

> **Note:** The MongoDB Search Playground performance might differ from production performance.

sections.heading: Try the Examples in the MongoDB Search Playground
sections.body:
You can try the following MongoDB Search features in a preconfigured
environment in the [MongoDB Search Playground](https://search-playground.mongodb.com/tools/code-sandbox/snapshots/new).

| MongoDB Search Feature | Description                                                                                                                             | Examples                                                                                      |
| ---------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| compound Operator      | Combines two or more operators into a single query.                                                                                     | must and mustNotmust and shouldCompound ScoreminimumShouldMatchfilter$match ReplacementNested |
| exists Operator        | Tests if a path to a specified indexed field name exists in a document.                                                                 | BasicEmbedded fieldCompound                                                                   |
| highlight Option       | Adds fields to the result set that display search terms as they appear in the returned documents, along with any adjacent text content. | BasicAdvancedMulti-FieldWildcardCompoundAutocomplete                                          |
